D10 Imaging L3 Apply Reference

Manual Kelvin + Tint

the two-knob model: temperature slides along the Planckian locus, tint slides perpendicular along the Duv axis

Raw converters expose white balance as two orthogonal axes. Temperature (K) moves the assumed illuminant along the Planckian locus — low K means warm cast (camera applies blue gain), high K means cool cast (camera applies red gain). Tint moves perpendicular to the locus along the Duv axis — positive tint means magenta cast (camera applies green gain), negative tint means green cast.

The chromaticity plot shows where the camera is currently placing its “white”. Drag the K slider and the marker slides along the curved Planckian locus; drag tint and it slides off-axis. The scene patches at right show the corresponding gain applied.
